reforms.description: The Czech government has introduced targeted tax relief for R&D spending, including plans to increase the maximum tax deduction on R&D expenditures, aimed at encouraging innovation and enhancing incentives for private sector innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing tax incentives, the reform is expected to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax relief measures have been put into effect as part of the government's fiscal reform programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances the existing tax framework to support R&D but does not constitute a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The corporate-tax system includes targeted tax relief tilted towards poorer regions and favourable treatment of R&D spending as well as special support to small-and-medium enterprises.

reforms.description: The Czech government is working to enhance the commercial application of scientific research by removing barriers that prevent academics from establishing businesses and intends to implement an 'innovation strategy' to support small and medium enterprises (SMEs) through increased coordination and financial assistance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the establishment of businesses by academics and providing support for SMEs, the reform is expected to foster innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively worked on removing barriers and enhancing support for SMEs as part of its innovation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to improve the startup ecosystem but does not constitute a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Barriers to academics setting up a business should be removed to allow greater opportunities for direct management and financial interest in commercial applications in science and technology.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Industry and Trade has launched three R&D programmes aimed at promoting research in new materials, strengthening links between business and the scientific community, and providing financial support for engineering processes and materials.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These programmes are designed to enhance collaboration between industry and research institutions, leading to innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes have been actively launched and are in operation to support R&D activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The launch of these programmes represents a significant investment in R&D but is not a complete overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Industry and Trade has launched three R&D programmes this year.
